summary: The session focused on designing an app similar to Getaround, discussing user interface design, backend interaction, communication with cars, and security measures. The user, an Android developer with 6 years of experience, sought detailed explanations on app architecture, including layers and technologies required for development.
facts: The user has 6 years of experience as an Android developer. The app design discussed is similar to Getaround, which involves renting cars. The app's presentation layer would use Android-specific UI components like Activities and Fragments. The application layer would implement business logic using Android libraries such as Retrofit for network communication and Room for local data storage. The domain layer would define domain objects and business rules using Java or Kotlin classes. The data layer would manage data storage with SQLite databases and cloud-based services like Amazon S3. The backend layer would handle server-side logic and communication with external systems using RESTful APIs and cloud infrastructure like AWS or Google Cloud Platform. Technologies mentioned for the app include Android SDK, Kotlin, RxJava, Dagger 2, Retrofit, and Room. Security measures discussed include two-factor authentication, identity verification, encrypted communication, secure storage, and access control.
